topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a jarring splash of unwelcome familiarity. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscle twitching beneath my skin. It was him. Mark. The boy who used to make my stomach clench just thinking about his shadow. A friend request. A deliberate, calculated move. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in short, sharp bursts. Every nerve ending seemed to be screaming. I didn't even bother to tap it. Instead, I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet room. I walked to the window, gripping the frame until my knuckles were white, watching the mundane world outside as if it were a sanctuary from the sudden, unwelcome intrusion.

[story 2]

When the alert flashed across my phone, I almost dropped it. My hands were shaking, a low, hot tremor running through my arms. It was Mark. Of course it was. The memory of his sneer, the way he’d cornered me by the lockers, flooded my mind, making my chest feel tight and constricted. I let out a slow, controlled exhale, trying to steady the frantic rhythm of my heart. I picked up the device, my fingers moving with deliberate slowness, and navigated to the request. A cold knot formed in my gut. I didn't want this. I didn't want any connection to that kind of history. I deleted the request immediately, the action swift and final, a silent rejection aimed directly at the source of the unwelcome message.

[story 3]

I found myself frozen, staring at the screen as the notification blinked. A slow burn started low in my abdomen, escalating quickly into a simmering heat that made my face feel flushed. Mark. That name alone was enough to send a spike of pure antagonism through me. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight. I could feel the tension coiling in my shoulders, a rigid defense mechanism kicking in. My mouth felt dry, and when I finally managed to type, the words were clipped, sharp, laced with an unmistakable edge of pure refusal. I hit send before I could second-guess the venom I was about to unleash.
